food_subgroup: Fish-based patties and cutlets with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Nyonya otah cutlet specifically; fish cake food product used as closest parent. The dish includes spiced fish patties served with mashed potatoes, baked beans, mixed salad, and brown sauce.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM (Fish cake) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Nyonya otah-style spiced fish cutlet with accompaniments.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Nyonya otah cutlet or sufficiently close parent concept in clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, comprising two otah fish patties (approximately 120 g total) with mashed potatoes (approximately 120 g), baked beans (approximately 80 g), mixed salad (approximately 50 g), and brown sauce (approximately 30 g).

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: HPB Singapore Foodo (otah/otak-otak, mashed potato, baked beans) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (mixed salad, brown sauce analogue)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Nyonya Otah Cutlet (Double Patty) as a composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: otah/otak-otak per HPB Foodo (fish-based spiced cake), mashed potato per HPB Foodo, baked beans per HPB Foodo, mixed salad and brown sauce per USDA SR Legacy analogues. Per-100 g values are derived from the estimated 400 g composite serving. Values are moderate-confidence estimates.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed fish-based meal with potato and legume components — analogous estimate based on: (1) fish cake GI ~40 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) mashed potato GI ~83 (Atkinson FS et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); (3) baked beans GI ~40 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI estimated at ~52.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nyonya Otah Cutlet (Double Patty). Composite GI estimated by weighting carbohydrate contributions from each component: mashed potato (~120 g, CHO ~17 g, GI 83), baked beans (~80 g, CHO ~12 g, GI 40), otah patties (~120 g, CHO ~4 g, GI ~40), salad/sauce (~80 g, CHO ~5 g, GI ~35). Weighted GI = (17×83 + 12×40 + 4×40 + 5×35) / 38 ≈ 52. GL per 400 g serving = (52 × 38) / 100 = 19.8, rounded to 20.0. Note: GL recalculated as (52 × 50) / 100 = 26.0 using total carbohydrate_g per serving (400 g × 12.5 g/100 g = 50 g). gi_category is low per ≤55 threshold.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1240 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a full serving of this dish provides approximately 1240 mg sodium (~54% of the 2300 mg daily limit), primarily from the otah seasoning, baked beans, and brown sauce; portion reduction or requesting sauce on the side is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~26 per serving) driven by mashed potato component
type2_diabetes.guidance: The mashed potato component raises the glycemic load of this dish to approximately 26 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider requesting a smaller potato portion or substituting with a lower-GI starch such as sweet potato to better manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g; ~8.4 g per serving) from coconut milk in otah and brown sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut milk used in Nyonya otah contributes approximately 8.4 g saturated fat per serving (~42% of the 20 g daily limit for a 2000 kcal diet); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption, and note that the fish component provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids that partially offset cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nyonya Otah Cutlet (Double Patty) is a Peranakan-Singaporean fusion dish served at casual dining establishments in Singapore. It consists of two pan-fried or grilled otah (otak-otak) fish patties — made from spiced fish paste blended with coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, chilli, and kaffir lime leaves — plated alongside creamy mashed potatoes, baked beans in tomato sauce, a mixed green salad, and brown gravy sauce. The otah patties have a firm yet moist texture with a fragrant, mildly spicy, and coconut-rich flavour profile characteristic of Peranakan (Nyonya) cuisine. The dish represents a fusion of traditional Malay-Chinese Peranakan cooking with a Western-style cutlet plate presentation. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~29 g per serving) from the fish patties, moderate carbohydrates (~50 g per serving) primarily from mashed potato and baked beans, and moderate fat (~19 g per serving) with saturated fat from coconut milk. Sodium content is elevated (~1240 mg per serving) due to seasoning in the otah, baked beans, and brown sauce. The composite glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~26) due to the mashed potato component. Clinically, individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size, particularly the mashed potato and sauce components. The fish base provides omega-3 fatty acids and high-quality protein, making it a nutritionally preferable protein source compared to red meat cutlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Nyonya otah cutlet double patty?
Is otah cutlet suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of otah cutlet?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis using HPB Singapore Foodo (otah/otak-otak, mashed potato, baked beans) and USDA SR Legacy (salad, brown sauce analogue). GI is an analogous composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the dish composition is well-described.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy; no direct laboratory analysis of this specific restaurant dish is available. 2. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on the described components (double patty + mashed potato + baked beans + salad + sauce); actual restaurant portion may vary. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimate. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 6. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 7. Halal status is assumed based on the fish-based composition and typical Peranakan restaurant practice in Singapore; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 8. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Meat and alternatives' as the primary protein component is fish-based otah patties; the mashed potato and baked beans are accompaniments. 9. Cuisine tags in the input (Ramen, Pizza, Pasta) appear to be platform-level restaurant tags unrelated to this specific dish and have not been carried forward.

Added 'Nyonya Otah Cutlet' to the flat 'aliases' array to ensure perfect consistency. Confirmed GI category 'low' correct for GI=52 (≤55 threshold).
